title: TraceMap Home
summary: Start with the deterministic static evidence boundary, then follow routes to proof, limitations, docs, and demo pages.
limitations: Home-page copy is orientation, not the source of proof.
Claims must route to public evidence pages or repository documents.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production usage, deployment state, endpoint performance, or release approval proof.
No AI impact analysis, embeddings, vector databases, or prompt-based classification in core TraceMap.

title: Capabilities
summary: Demo-level capability map for scanner, reducer, report, language adapter, and review-packet surfaces with status labels.
limitations: Capability rows are status-framed and should not be upgraded beyond their supporting public proof.
Future and dev-only rows remain bounded until main evidence exists.
nonClaims: No release approval, operational safety, endpoint performance, or deployment state proof.
No raw source snippets, raw SQL, config values, secrets, facts.ndjson, index.sqlite, or logs/analyzer.log are published.

title: Docs
summary: Public documentation hub linking source-of-truth repository docs, validation notes, acceptance criteria, and packet guidance.
limitations: The docs hub is navigation; repository docs remain the source-of-truth references.
Readers should inspect validation and limitations before summarizing product behavior.
nonClaims: No runtime behavior, production usage, release approval, or absence-of-impact proof.
No AI impact analysis or LLM-based product feature claim.

title: Evidence Model
summary: Explains rule IDs, evidence tiers, file spans, coverage labels, and why public conclusions need supporting proof.
limitations: Static evidence can show source relationships and gaps, not operational behavior.
Tier labels describe evidence strength and analysis gaps.
nonClaims: No production traffic, branch feasibility, deployment state, endpoint performance, or release approval proof.
No prompt-based classification or AI impact analysis in the scanner or reducer.

limitations: The register records follow-up rows and stop conditions; it is not scanner output, reducer output, validation success, or a public proof source.
Gap rows must keep what evidence exists, what cannot be concluded, next owner, proof or validation route, safe wording, and stop condition attached.

No raw facts, raw SQLite content, analyzer logs, raw source snippets, raw SQL, config values, secrets, local paths, raw remotes, generated scan directories, private sample names, raw command output, hidden validation details, or credential-like values are public gap-register material.
